title: Docker Setup for ggvolc

code: # latest build from master docker pull ghcr.io/loukesio/ggvolc:latest  # a specific release docker pull ghcr.io/loukesio/ggvolc:0.3.0  # run RStudio Server on http://localhost:8787 (user: rstudio, password: ggvolc123) docker run --rm -p 8787:8787 -e PASSWORD=ggvolc123 ghcr.io/loukesio/ggvolc:latest

title: Docker Setup for ggvolc
text: Build start container: Access RStudio Server: Open browser go : http://localhost:8787 Username: rstudio Password: ggvolc123 (can changed docker-compose.yml) Start using ggvolc: Stop container:
code: docker-compose up -d library(ggvolc)  # Load example data data(all_genes) data(attention_genes)  # Create a volcano plot ggvolc(all_genes, attention_genes, add_seg = TRUE) docker-compose down

title: Docker Setup for ggvolc
text: Build image: Run container: Access RStudio Server: Go http://localhost:8787 Login username rstudio password ggvolc123 Stop remove container:
code: docker build -t ggvolc:latest . docker run -d \   --name ggvolc-rstudio \   -p 8787:8787 \   -e PASSWORD=ggvolc123 \   -v $(pwd)/workspace:/home/rstudio/workspace \   ggvolc:latest docker stop ggvolc-rstudio docker rm ggvolc-rstudio

title: Docker Publishing Setup
text: guide explains enable automatic Docker image publishing Docker Hub GitHub Container Registry push changes.

title: Docker Publishing Setup
text: ✅ Automated Building & Testing - Already enabled! - Builds Docker image every push master/main - Runs tests verify ggvolc works correctly - Generates build reports

title: Docker Publishing Setup
text: Go https://hub.docker.com/signup Create account (free tier fine) Go https://hub.docker.com/repositories Click “Create Repository” Name: ggvolc Visibility: Public (Private prefer) Go Account Settings → Security → Access Tokens Click “New Access Token” Description: “GitHub Actions” Permissions: Read & Write Copy token (won’t see !) Go GitHub repo: Settings → Secrets variables → Actions Click “New repository secret” Name: DOCKER_USERNAME, Value: Docker Hub username Name: DOCKER_PASSWORD, Value: access token step 3 Edit .github/workflows/docker-build.yml Uncomment push--dockerhub job (lines ~73-108) Update loukesio/ggvolc YOUR_USERNAME/ggvolc Commit push

title: Docker Publishing Setup
text: Docker image automatically published : docker pull YOUR_USERNAME/ggvolc:latest

title: Getting started with ggvolc
text: package ships two data frames. all_genes complete DESeq2-style result table; attention_genes small subset genes might want highlight.

title: Getting started with ggvolc
text: Passing single data frame colours every gene significance. default gene called significant adjusted p-value (FDR) 0.05 |log2FoldChange| exceeds 1.

title: Getting started with ggvolc
text: Supply second data frame genes drawn black outline labelled.  add_seg = TRUE argument adds dashed guides fold-change significance thresholds.

title: Getting started with ggvolc
text: usually want build separate data frame just label handful hits. label_top picks significant genes , label_dir controls direction drawn .   label_dir accepts "" (default), "", "", "".
code: # the 10 most significant genes overall ggvolc(all_genes, label_top = 10, add_seg = TRUE, title = "Top 10 hits") # the top 8 up- and top 8 down-regulated genes ggvolc(all_genes, label_top = 8, label_dir = "each",        add_seg = TRUE, title = "Top 8 up + 8 down")

what: Calling significance on the FDR (or the raw p-value)
title: Getting started with ggvolc
text: ggvolc() calls significance adjusted p-value (padj) default, right cutoff DE workflows. y-axis significance line follow choice, plot stays internally consistent. Switch raw p-value sig_col = "pvalue".  ggvolc() also robust p-values exactly 0 (DESeq2 edgeR can emit strongest genes): rather dropping points, -log10 value capped stay near top plot.
code: ggvolc(all_genes, sig_col = "pvalue", add_seg = TRUE,        title = "Significance on the raw p-value")

title: Getting started with ggvolc
text: Point size can encode either fold change significance.

title: Getting started with ggvolc
text: genes_table() composes volcano plot gt table highlighted genes using patchwork. result single object can save ggplot2::ggsave().
code: p <- ggvolc(all_genes, attention_genes, add_seg = TRUE) genes_table(p, attention_genes)

what: Works with DESeq2, edgeR, and limma
title: Getting started with ggvolc
text: Column names three pipelines detected mapped automatically, can pass output straight . Gene identifiers held row names (edgeR limma often ) promoted genes column . package ships edger_genes, example topTags()-style table gene identifiers row names, can try right away:
code: data(edger_genes) head(edger_genes, 3) #>           logFC  logCPM       PValue          FDR #> GCR1   2.244064 12.8143 4.434241e-29 2.153711e-25 #> OPI10 -2.257454  9.9807 4.880607e-27 1.185255e-23 #> AGA2   3.829474  7.9665 4.143136e-26 6.707736e-23  ggvolc(edger_genes, label_top = 8, add_seg = TRUE, title = "edgeR input")

title: Getting started with ggvolc
text: optional ggiraph package installed, interactive = TRUE returns widget hovering point reveals gene name statistics.

title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: Install package using following commands
code: # for now, you can install the developmental version of ltc # first you need to install the devtools package # in case you have not already installed install.packages("devtools") # and load it library(devtools)  # then you can install the dev version of the ltc devtools::install_github("loukesio/ggvolc") # and load it library(ggvolc)

title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: ggvolc turns results differential-expression analysis — DESeq2, edgeR, limma — clean, publication-ready volcano plot. can highlight genes, auto-label top hits, attach gt table, make whole thing interactive. Start loading library exploring two example datasets ship package: Created 2023-08-11 reprex v2.0.2

title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: Pass single data frame every gene coloured significance — , , significant.

title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: Supply second data frame outline label genes care .

what: 3. Add segments to indicate areas of significance.
title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: Turn add_seg draw fold-change significance thresholds.
code: ggvolc(all_genes, attention_genes, add_seg = TRUE) +   labs(title="Add segments of significance")

what: 4. Indicate the size of point based on the log2FoldChange column.
title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: Scale point size effect size size_var = "log2FoldChange".

what: 5. Indicate the size of the point based on the pvalue.
title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: scale significance size_var = "pvalue".

what: 6. Add a table with the genes of interest.
title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: gene table rendered gt composed patchwork, result single object can style save ggplot2::ggsave().
code: plot <- ggvolc(all_genes, attention_genes, add_seg = TRUE) +   labs(title="Add a table with the genes of interest")  plot %>%   genes_table(attention_genes)

what: 7. Works with DESeq2, edgeR, and limma out of the box
title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: ggvolc() genes_table() accept output three major differential-expression pipelines directly — column names auto-detected mapped internally, manual renaming needed. Gene identifiers held row names (edgeR/limma often ) promoted genes column automatically. package ships edger_genes, example topTags()-style table, can see directly. analysis pass real thing:
code: # edgeR: pass topTags()$table straight in edger_res <- as.data.frame(edgeR::topTags(qlf, n = Inf)) ggvolc(edger_res) data(edger_genes)          # an edgeR topTags()-style table (genes in the rownames) head(edger_genes, 3) #>           logFC  logCPM       PValue          FDR #> GCR1   2.244064 12.8143 4.434241e-29 2.153711e-25 #> OPI10 -2.257454  9.9807 4.880607e-27 1.185255e-23 #> AGA2   3.829474  7.9665 4.143136e-26 6.707736e-23  ggvolc(edger_genes, label_top = 8, add_seg = TRUE, title = "edgeR input")

what: 8. Significance on the adjusted p-value (FDR) — the default
title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: ggvolc() calls significance adjusted p-value (FDR) default — right cutoff DE workflows — y-axis significance line follow along, plot stays consistent. Prefer raw p-value? Set sig_col = "pvalue".  table adjusted-p column, ggvolc automatically falls back raw p-value. ggvolc() also robust p-values exactly 0 (DESeq2/edgeR can emit strongest genes): instead silently dropping , -log10 value capped stay pinned near top plot.
code: ggvolc(all_genes, add_seg = TRUE)   # significance on padj (FDR) by default

title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: need build separate data frame — label_top highlights labels N significant genes , label_dir lets pick direction.   label_dir accepts "" (default), "", "", "".
code: # top 10 overall ggvolc(all_genes, label_top = 10, add_seg = TRUE, title = "Top 10 hits") # top 8 up- and top 8 down-regulated ggvolc(all_genes, label_top = 8, label_dir = "each", add_seg = TRUE,        title = "Top 8 up + 8 down")

title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: Set interactive = TRUE get ggiraph widget — hover point see gene name statistics. ggiraph optional dependency (install install.packages("ggiraph")). Note: GitHub can’t run widget, image static preview. live, hover--inspect version runs RStudio package website.

title: Create Volcano Plots for Differential Gene Expression Data
text: 📖 Package website — full function reference getting-started article every example (including live interactive volcano). 🐳 Docker image — docker pull ghcr.io/loukesio/ggvolc:latest ready--run RStudio environment ggvolc pre-installed. 🐛 Issues & questions — bug reports feature requests welcome.

title: Gene Expression Analysis Dataset — all_genes
text: dataset containing gene expression analysis results. seven columns capturing various statistics related gene expression.

title: Gene Expression Analysis Dataset — all_genes
text: data frame following columns: genes Character. Gene name identifier. baseMean Numeric. base mean value gene across samples. log2FoldChange Numeric. log2 fold change gene expression. Positive values indicate upregulation negative values indicate downregulation. lfcSE Numeric. Standard error log2 fold change. stat Numeric. Wald statistic gene's expression change. pvalue Numeric. Raw p-value test gene's expression change. padj Numeric. Adjusted p-value multiple testing corrections.

title: Attention Genes Dataset — attention_genes
text: dataset containing specific genes interest referred "attention genes".

title: Attention Genes Dataset — attention_genes
text: data frame 10 rows 7 variables: genes Character. Gene name identifier. baseMean Numeric vector: Base mean expression level genes log2FoldChange Numeric vector: Log2 Fold Change gene expression lfcSE Numeric vector: Standard error log2 fold change stat Numeric vector: Wald statistic gene's expression change pvalue Numeric vector: Raw p-value Wald test padj Numeric vector: Adjusted p-value multiple testing using Benjamini-Hochberg procedure

what: Detect DE pipeline from column names — detect_de_source
title: Detect DE pipeline from column names — detect_de_source
text: Detect DE pipeline column names

title: Detect DE pipeline from column names — detect_de_source
Detect DE pipeline from column names — detect_de_source
text: cols Character vector column names.

title: Detect DE pipeline from column names — detect_de_source
text: One "deseq2", "edger", "limma",   errors unrecognized.

title: edgeR-style Differential Expression Dataset — edger_genes
text: experiment all_genes, re-expressed column conventions edgeR's topTags() output. gene identifiers stored row names (topTags() returns ), lets demonstrate ggvolc accepts edgeR results directly promotes rowname gene IDs genes column automatically.

title: edgeR-style Differential Expression Dataset — edger_genes
text: data frame gene identifiers row names following   columns: logFC Numeric. Log2 fold change gene expression. logCPM Numeric. Average log2 counts per million. PValue Numeric. Raw p-value differential expression test. FDR Numeric. Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted p-value (false discovery rate).

what: Combine a Volcano Plot with a gt Table of Genes — genes_table
title: Combine a Volcano Plot with a gt Table of Genes — genes_table
text: Takes ggplot object (typically ggvolc) data frame gene details produces combined layout using patchwork. gene table rendered polished gt table color-coded significance formatted numeric columns.

title: Combine a Volcano Plot with a gt Table of Genes — genes_table
Combine a Volcano Plot with a gt Table of Genes — genes_table
text: plot_obj ggplot object, typically output ggvolc. data2 data frame containing gene details. Columns auto-detected way ggvolc (DESeq2, edgeR, limma conventions). Required columns mapping: genes, log2FoldChange, pvalue. Optional: baseMean, padj. p_value Significance threshold used color-coding p-value column. Default 0.05. table_height Relative height table panel (plot panel always 3). Default 1.

title: Combine a Volcano Plot with a gt Table of Genes — genes_table
text: patchwork object can composed saved   ggplot2::ggsave().

title: Combine a Volcano Plot with a gt Table of Genes — genes_table
code: # Load example datasets data(all_genes) data(attention_genes)  # Create a volcano plot and combine it with a gt gene table p <- ggvolc(all_genes, attention_genes, add_seg = TRUE) genes_table(p, attention_genes)

what: Print Welcome Message and ASCII Art upon Loading `ggvolc` — ggvolc-onAttach
title: Print Welcome Message and ASCII Art upon Loading `ggvolc` — ggvolc-onAttach
text: function executed `ggvolc` package attached R session. prints welcome message ASCII representation related package.

title: Print Welcome Message and ASCII Art upon Loading `ggvolc` — ggvolc-onAttach
code: # The function is automatically called when you use: # library(ggvolc)

title: Create a Volcano Plot — ggvolc
text: function creates volcano plot using ggplot2 based provided datasets. particularly useful visualizing differential gene expression data.

title: Create a Volcano Plot — ggvolc
code: ggvolc(   data1,   data2 = NULL,   size_var = NULL,   p_value = 0.05,   fc = 1,   sig_col = c("padj", "pvalue"),   label_top = NULL,   label_dir = c("both", "up", "down", "each"),   title = NULL,   interactive = FALSE,   not_sig_color = "#808080",   down_reg_color = "#00798c",   up_reg_color = "#d1495b",   add_seg = FALSE )

title: Create a Volcano Plot — ggvolc
text: data1 Data frame primary dataset. Accepts output DESeq2 (results()), edgeR (topTags()), limma (topTable()). Gene identifiers can live genes column row names. data2 Data frame secondary dataset (genes interest). Uses auto-detection data1. Default NULL. size_var Variable determining size points. Options "log2FoldChange" "pvalue". Default NULL (fixed size). p_value Threshold statistical significance. Default 0.05. fc Fold change threshold determining upregulated downregulated genes. Default 1. sig_col Column used call significance build y-axis. Either "padj" (adjusted p-value / FDR, default recommended cutoff calling hits DE workflows) "pvalue" (raw p-value). y-axis significance segment follow choice, plot stays internally consistent. default "padj" used data adjusted-p column, ggvolc falls back raw p-value. label_top Optional integer. supplied, label_top significant genes (smallest sig_col, among significant genes) highlighted labelled automatically, without building separate data2. Combined data2 given. Default NULL. label_dir Direction draw label_top genes . One "" (top N significant genes, default), "" (top N upregulated), "" (top N downregulated), "" (top N upregulated top N downregulated, .e. 2N labels). Ignored label_top NULL. title Plot title. Default NULL (title). interactive Logical. TRUE, returns interactive ggiraph girafe widget hovering point reveals gene name statistics. Requires optional ggiraph package. Default FALSE. not_sig_color Color non-significant genes. Default "#808080". down_reg_color Color downregulated genes. Default "#00798c". up_reg_color Color upregulated genes. Default "#d1495b". add_seg Logical. TRUE, dashed lines added plot indicating p-value fold change thresholds. Default FALSE.

title: Create a Volcano Plot — ggvolc
text: ggplot2 object displaying volcano plot, ,   interactive = TRUE, ggiraph girafe widget.

title: Create a Volcano Plot — ggvolc
text: Column names DESeq2, edgeR, limma automatically detected mapped internally, can pass output three pipelines directly.

title: Create a Volcano Plot — ggvolc
code: # Load example datasets included in the package data(all_genes) data(attention_genes)  # Create a basic volcano plot with default settings # Points are colored by significance (p < 0.05, |log2FC| > 1) ggvolc(all_genes)   # Highlight specific genes of interest with labels # These genes are shown with black borders and gene names ggvolc(all_genes, attention_genes)   # Add dashed lines to indicate significance thresholds ggvolc(all_genes, attention_genes, add_seg = TRUE)   # Significance is called on the adjusted p-value (FDR) by default; # use the raw p-value instead with: ggvolc(all_genes, sig_col = "pvalue")   # Automatically label the 10 most significant genes ggvolc(all_genes, label_top = 10)   # Label the top 10 upregulated genes only ggvolc(all_genes, label_top = 10, label_dir = "up")   # Label the top 8 of each direction (up to 16 labels) ggvolc(all_genes, label_top = 8, label_dir = "each")   # Give the plot a title ggvolc(all_genes, title = "Treated vs. control")   # Customize colors for up- and down-regulated genes ggvolc(all_genes, attention_genes,        up_reg_color = "#E63946",        down_reg_color = "#457B9D")   # Scale point size by p-value instead of default ggvolc(all_genes, attention_genes, size_var = "pvalue")   # Adjust significance thresholds (p-value and fold change) ggvolc(all_genes, p_value = 0.01, fc = 2)   # edgeR-style input works directly edger_df <- data.frame(   genes = paste0("gene", 1:50),   logFC = rnorm(50, 0, 2),   logCPM = runif(50, 5, 15),   PValue = 10^-runif(50, 0, 6),   FDR = 10^-runif(50, 0, 5) ) ggvolc(edger_df)   # limma-style input works too limma_df <- data.frame(   genes = paste0("gene", 1:50),   logFC = rnorm(50, 0, 2),   AveExpr = runif(50, 5, 15),   t = rnorm(50),   P.Value = 10^-runif(50, 0, 6),   adj.P.Val = 10^-runif(50, 0, 5) ) ggvolc(limma_df)   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Interactive volcano (requires the ggiraph package): hover a point to # see the gene name and its statistics ggvolc(all_genes, attention_genes, interactive = TRUE) } # }

what: Compute -log10(p) with infinite values capped — neglog10_cap
title: Compute -log10(p) with infinite values capped — neglog10_cap
text: -log10() p-value exactly 0 (DESeq2 edgeR can emit floating-point underflow strongest genes) Inf, ggplot2 silently drops non-finite values. make significant genes disappear plot. helper replaces infinite values finite ceiling genes stay plot, near top.

title: Compute -log10(p) with infinite values capped — neglog10_cap
Compute -log10(p) with infinite values capped — neglog10_cap
text: p Numeric vector p-values. ceiling Optional finite value used capping. NULL set 1.05 times largest finite -log10(p). Pass explicit value keep two datasets (e.g. background highlighted genes) scale.

title: Compute -log10(p) with infinite values capped — neglog10_cap
text: list value (capped -log10(p) vector),   ceiling (cap used), n_capped (many values   replaced).

what: Detect DE source and standardize column names — standardize_de_columns
title: Detect DE source and standardize column names — standardize_de_columns
text: Inspects column names data frame maps internal ggvolc convention: genes, log2FoldChange, pvalue, padj, baseMean.

title: Detect DE source and standardize column names — standardize_de_columns
Detect DE source and standardize column names — standardize_de_columns
text: df data frame DE analysis pipeline.

title: Detect DE source and standardize column names — standardize_de_columns
text: data frame standardized column names.

title: Detect DE source and standardize column names — standardize_de_columns
text: Recognized formats: DESeq2 log2FoldChange, pvalue, padj, baseMean edgeR logFC, PValue, FDR, logCPM limma logFC, P.Value, adj.P.Val, AveExpr

title: ggvolc 0.3.0
text: Significance adjusted p-value (FDR) default. ggvolc() gains sig_col argument ("padj" "pvalue") now defaults "padj", hits called FDR — recommended cutoff DE workflows — y-axis significance segment following choice plot stays internally consistent. Set sig_col = "pvalue" raw p-value. default "padj" used data adjusted-p column, ggvolc falls back raw p-value automatically. Robust p == 0. DESeq2 edgeR can report p-value exactly 0 (floating-point underflow) strongest genes; -log10(0) Inf, ggplot2 silently drops — significant genes used vanish plot. values now capped finite ceiling (just significant real gene) message reports many adjusted. label_top = N. Automatically highlight label N significant genes without building separate data2. Combines explicit data2 supplied. label_dir. Choose direction labelled genes drawn : "" (default), "", "", "" (top N upregulated top N downregulated). Pairs label_top, e.g. label_top = 10, label_dir = "". title argument. plot title now configurable defaults NULL (title), replacing previous hardcoded placeholder title. Interactive volcano plots. ggvolc(..., interactive = TRUE) returns ggiraph girafe widget hovering point reveals gene name statistics. ggiraph optional (Suggests) dependency, core package still installs without . New internal helper: neglog10_cap(). README now generated README.Rmd (via devtools::build_readme()) every example plot rendered live code kept sync package. DESCRIPTION now declares R (>= 4.1.0), matching native pipe (|>) used internally (previously mis-declared >= 3.5.0).

title: ggvolc 0.2.0
text: DESeq2: log2FoldChange, pvalue, padj, baseMean edgeR: logFC, PValue, FDR, logCPM limma: logFC, P.Value, adj.P.Val, AveExpr Gene identifiers stored row names (common edgeR/limma) automatically promoted genes column. Replaces gridExtra::grid.arrange patchwork::wrap_table() Gene table rendered gt table formatted numerics, color-coded p-values, directional log2FC coloring p-value / adjusted-p columns use grid-safe scientific notation (1.0e-08) exponents render correctly table composed plot graphic patchwork Returns proper patchwork object (composable, ggsave()-able) New internal helpers: standardize_de_columns(), detect_de_source() Added testthat test suite covering three DE formats, column detection, attention genes, plot options, new gene table. Dependencies: replaced gridExtra gt patchwork. Version bump 0.2.0.

title: ggvolc 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-16

title: ggvolc 0.1.0
text: Initial release ggvolc package Create customizable volcano plots differential gene expression analysis ggvolc(): Main function create volcano plots genes_table(): Combine volcano plots gene tables Highlight genes interest Adjustable significance thresholds (p-value fold change) Customizable colors /regulated genes Optional significance segment lines Size scaling log2FoldChange p-value Integration ggplot2 additional customization all_genes: Complete differential expression results attention_genes: Subset genes highlight
